short: Ayo Owolabi is a technical founder building a portfolio of products at the intersection of AI and operator tooling.
medium: Ayo Owolabi is a technical founder and operator. He builds products for people who do high-leverage work independently — content engines, research tools, and developer infrastructure. He writes about building with AI, the craft of solo product development, and where technology is creating new possibilities for small operators.
long:
Ayo Owolabi is a technical founder building a portfolio of products at the intersection of AI and solo operator tooling. His current projects include Scarab HQ, a content engine that takes ideas from raw notes to published output without friction; Legworker, a research automation tool for people who do their own due diligence; and Bedrock, which provides infrastructure and tooling for early-stage technical teams.

He writes about what it means to build with AI, the economics and craft of solo product development, and the structural changes in technology that are creating new possibilities for small operators who can leverage code and AI as force multipliers.

Previously, he worked across early-stage startups in engineering and product roles. He is based in London.
